name: Carrascalejo from Guadalupe – circular through the Parque Natural Geoparque Villuercas-Ibores Jara
name_original: Carrascalejo desde Guadalupe – circular por el Parque Natural Geoparque Villuercas-Ibores Jara
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Guadalupe traverse a landscape characterized by the Sierra de Guadalupe mountain range, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region features significant elevation changes, with routes often climbing through hills and valleys. Cyclists can expect to encounter natural parks and geologically interesting areas, providing a diverse backdrop for rides. The network of routes caters to those seeking challenging ascents and longer distances. Best road cycling routes around Guadalupe The most popular road cycling route is Cañamero from Guadalupe – circular by Mirador del pico Villuercas, a 38.0 miles (61.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 59 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain and panoramic views from Mirador del pico Villuercas. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Carrascalejo from Guadalupe – circular through the Parque Natural Geoparque Villuercas-Ibores Jara, a difficult 69.8 miles (112.4 km) path. This route explores the diverse terrain of the Parque Natural Geoparque Villuercas-Ibores Jara, offering a long and challenging ride. Local road cyclists also love the Charco de la Nutria from Guadalupe – circular via Castillo de Cabañas and Castañar de Ibor, a 76.2 miles (122.6 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including historical sites, often completed in about 6 hours 50 minutes. Road cycling around Guadalupe is defined by challenging mountain passes, natural park landscapes, and significant elevation changes. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Guadalupe are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Guadalupe's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Guadalupe?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Guadalupe, offering a diverse range of experiences. The majority of these, about two-thirds, are classified as difficult, while the remaining routes are moderate in difficulty.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Guadalupe?
A: Road cycling routes around Guadalupe are characterized by the Sierra de Guadalupe mountain range, featuring varied terrain with significant elevation changes. You can expect challenging climbs through hills and valleys, often passing through natural parks and geologically interesting areas.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Guadalupe?
A: While Guadalupe is known for its challenging routes, there are several moderate options available. These routes still involve elevation changes but are less demanding than the difficult ones, making them suitable for cyclists looking for a less strenuous experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058594184/city-tour-through-guadalupe-circular-walk-through-the-city-s-viewpoints" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City Tour through Guadalupe – circular walk through the city's viewpoints&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that offers a good introduction to the area's cycling.

Q: What are some advanced road cycling routes in Guadalupe?
A: Guadalupe is ideal for advanced road cyclists seeking a challenge. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058517030/carrascalejo-from-guadalupe-circular-through-the-parque-natural-geoparque-villuercas-ibores-jara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrascalejo from Guadalupe – circular through the Parque Natural Geoparque Villuercas-Ibores Jara&lt;/a&gt;, spanning nearly 70 miles with over 1800 meters of elevation gain, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058340185/charco-de-la-nutria-from-guadalupe-circular-via-castillo-de-cabanas-and-castanar-de-ibor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de la Nutria from Guadalupe – circular via Castillo de Cabañas and Castañar de Ibor&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 76 miles with more than 2100 meters of climbing, offer significant tests of endurance and skill.

Q: Are there any circular road bike routes in Guadalupe?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Guadalupe are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058550122/canamero-from-guadalupe-circular-by-mirador-del-pico-villuercas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañamero from Guadalupe – circular by Mirador del pico Villuercas&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058594184/city-tour-through-guadalupe-circular-walk-through-the-city-s-viewpoints" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City Tour through Guadalupe – circular walk through the city's viewpoints&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for exploration.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I find along road cycling routes in Guadalupe?
A: Road cycling routes in Guadalupe offer numerous scenic viewpoints. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058550122/canamero-from-guadalupe-circular-by-mirador-del-pico-villuercas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañamero from Guadalupe – circular by Mirador del pico Villuercas&lt;/a&gt; route provides panoramic views from the Mirador del pico Villuercas &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3406495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;(Pico Villuercas Summit)&lt;/a&gt;. Other routes may pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4253512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint at the Estrecho de Peña Amarilla&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning vistas of the natural landscape.

Q: What interesting landmarks can I see while road cycling around Guadalupe?
A: While road cycling around Guadalupe, you can encounter several historical and natural landmarks. Routes may pass by the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5717374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Monastery of Santa María de Guadalupe&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3847287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalupe Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;, or even the intriguing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4461596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Chiquita Cave&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058340185/charco-de-la-nutria-from-guadalupe-circular-via-castillo-de-cabanas-and-castanar-de-ibor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de la Nutria from Guadalupe – circular via Castillo de Cabañas and Castañar de Ibor&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, leads through varied landscapes including historical sites like Castillo de Cabañas.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Guadalupe?
A: The road cycling routes in Guadalupe are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the challenging mountain passes, the diverse natural park landscapes, and the significant elevation changes that make for a rewarding and memorable ride.

Q: Are there any natural parks or geological sites accessible by road bike in Guadalupe?
A: Yes, several road cycling routes in Guadalupe traverse natural parks and geologically significant areas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058517030/carrascalejo-from-guadalupe-circular-through-the-parque-natural-geoparque-villuercas-ibores-jara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrascalejo from Guadalupe – circular through the Parque Natural Geoparque Villuercas-Ibores Jara&lt;/a&gt; route explores the diverse terrain of this natural geopark, offering a unique cycling experience through protected landscapes.

Q: What is the longest road cycling route available in Guadalupe?
A: One of the longest road cycling routes in Guadalupe is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058340185/charco-de-la-nutria-from-guadalupe-circular-via-castillo-de-cabanas-and-castanar-de-ibor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de la Nutria from Guadalupe – circular via Castillo de Cabañas and Castañar de Ibor&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 76.2 miles (122.6 km) and features over 2100 meters of elevation gain, making it a substantial ride for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs in Guadalupe?
A: Yes, some routes in the Guadalupe area may offer views of local water bodies. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6042271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho del Fresno Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight in the region, and while not directly on a featured road cycling route, it indicates the presence of such natural features that might be incorporated into longer rides or explored nearby.
tips: Spectacular viewpoint from the south looking towards the old town to the north and the magnificent Royal Monastery of Our Lady of Guadalupe and its medieval walls and towers.

A railway bridge from the last century built as part of an ambiguous plan to connect Madrid and Huelva although like many projects in Spain it was never finished. Today, the impressive structure is part of the Vía Verde, an adventurous walking and cycling route.

Carrascalejo a small town in the region bordering the province of Castilla y León. The town is located at 600 meters above sea level on the slopes of the Villuercas Ibores La Jara Geopark Natural Park.

The views from here are a good reward for the climb. The site is full of references to the Camino de Guadalupe with a statue of a pilgrim, an iron cross, a milestone indicating the kilometer point and a post with the official logo indicating the way.

Narrow well-paved road with practically no vehicle traffic. Ascent from Guadalupe towards Carrascalejo, long but stretched with percentages that do not exceed 7%. The entire route is flanked by forests of oaks, cork oaks and chestnut trees. The landscape is spectacular with the Villuercas peak in the background.

This site is a fantastic prelude before entering Guadeloupe. From the top you can see the contrast between the monastery and the town center with the modern viaduct in the background and the entire valley. I imagine the thoughts of the pilgrims who arrived here before there were roads and I realize that, despite the difference in cultures, we are still the same humans.

The Royal Monastery of Santa María de Guadalupe is a 14th-century monastery located in the Spanish town of Guadalupe, in the province of Cáceres. It was declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1993. Inside, you can appreciate Gothic, Mudéjar, Renaissance, Baroque, and Neoclassical architectural styles.

A place with a rich history and breathtaking architectural beauty! 🌟🏰
